title: Two-stage dry-race legality model: `needs another stop before finish` then `pit next lap`
summary: The FIA rules require at least two dry-weather tyre specifications in a dry race unless inters/wets were used.
rationale: The FIA rules require at least two dry-weather tyre specifications in a dry race unless inters/wets were used. That means some cars must still stop even when naive finish-on-current-tyre logic says they can make the distance, especially `Stint==1` dry runners late in the race.
implementation_hint: Create a first head for `must_stop_before_finish` using dry/wet regime, stint count, estimated laps remaining, and whether current stint is plausibly the only dry specification used so far; then condition a next-lap classifier on that head or use its OOF score as a feature. Keep a separate path for wet/intermediate rows because the legal constraint changes. Test especially on late-race dry rows with `Stint==1`.
expected_effect: Should recover missed late-race mandatory-stop pressure that pure degradation features understate, improving ranking around one-stop vs no-stop edge cases.
risk: The dataset may not expose full compound history, so the legality state is partly inferred and can be noisy; Monaco has special wording and should be handled separately or excluded from this feature.
